heading: Levee safety initiative

Rehabilitation of Existing Levees, , , as amended by , , ; , , , provided that:Pub. L. 113–121, title III, § 3017128 Stat. 1300Pub. L. 117–263, div. H, title LXXXI, § 8382(a)136 Stat. 3828Pub. L. 118–272, div. A, title III, § 1303(i)138 Stat. 3115June 10, 2014Dec. 23, 2022Jan. 4, 2025“(a).—In GeneralThe Secretary [of the Army] shall carry out measures that address consolidation, settlement, subsidence, sea level rise, and new datum to restore federally authorized hurricane and storm damage reduction projects that were constructed as of the date of enactment of this Act [] to the authorized levels of protection of the projects if the Secretary determines the necessary work is technically feasible, environmentally acceptable, and economically justified.June 10, 2014“(b).—LimitationThis section shall only apply to those projects for which the executed project partnership agreement provides that the non-Federal interest is not required to perform future measures to restore the project to the authorized level of protection of the project to account for subsidence and sea-level rise as part of the operation, maintenance, repair, replacement, and rehabilitation responsibilities.“(c)Cost Share.—“(1).—In generalThe non-Federal share of the cost of construction of a project carried out under this section shall be determined as provided in subsections (a) through (d) of section 103 of the Water Resources Development Act of 1986 ().33 U.S.C. 2213“(2).—Certain activitiesThe non-Federal share of the cost of operations, maintenance, repair, replacement, and rehabilitation for a project carried out under this section shall be 100 percent.“(d).—Report to CongressNot later than 5 years after the date of enactment of this Act, the Secretary shall include in the annual report developed under section 7001 []—33 U.S.C. 2282d“(1)any recommendations relating to the continued need for the authority provided under this section;“(2)a description of the measures carried out under this section;“(3)any lessons learned relating to the measures implemented under this section; and“(4)best practices for carrying out measures to restore hurricane and storm damage reduction projects.“(e).—Termination of AuthorityThe authority of the Secretary under this section terminates on .”December 31, 2033
“Secretary” DefinedSecretary means the Secretary of the Army, see , set out as a note under .section 2 of Pub. L. 110–114section 2201 of this title

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: The Secretary, in consultation with the Administrator, shall carry out a levee safety initiative.
an administrator of the levee safety initiative; and
such staff as are necessary to implement the initiative.

children.label: A
children.text: are available for common, uniform use by all Federal, State, regional, local, and tribal agencies;

children.label: B
children.text: incorporate policies, procedures, standards, and criteria for a range of levee types, canal structures, and related facilities and features; and

children.label: C
children.text: provide for adaptation to local, regional, or watershed conditions.

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: 2 Requirement
children.text: The policies, procedures, standards, and criteria under paragraph (1)(B) shall be developed taking into consideration the levee hazard potential classification system established under subsection (d).

children.label: A
children.text: the activities and practices carried out by State, regional, local, and tribal governments and the private sector to safely build, regulate, operate, and maintain levees; and

children.label: B
children.text: Federal activities that facilitate State, regional, or tribal efforts to develop and implement effective State, regional, or tribal programs for the safety of levees, including levee inspection, levee rehabilitation, locally developed floodplain management, and public education and training programs.

children.heading: Consideration by Federal agencies
children.label: 4 Consideration by Federal agencies
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, all Federal agencies shall consider the levee safety guidelines in carrying out activities relating to the management of levees.

children.label: A
children.text: issue draft guidelines for public comment, including comment by States, regional districts, Indian tribes, non-Federal interests, and other appropriate stakeholders; and

children.label: B
children.text: consider any comments received in the development of final guidelines.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a hazard potential classification system for use under the levee safety initiative and participating programs.

children.heading: Revision
children.label: 2 Revision
children.text: The Secretary shall review and, as necessary, revise the hazard potential classification system not less frequently than once every 5 years.

children.heading: Consistency
children.label: 3 Consistency
children.text: The hazard potential classification system established pursuant to this subsection shall be consistent with and incorporated into the levee safety action classification tool developed by the Corps of Engineers.

children.label: A
children.text: developing levee safety programs;

children.label: B
children.text: identifying and reducing flood risks associated with levees;

children.label: C
children.text: identifying local actions that may be carried out to reduce flood risks in leveed areas; and

children.label: D
children.text: rehabilitating, improving, replacing, reconfiguring, modifying, and removing levees and levee systems.

children.label: A
children.text: be in the process of establishing or have in effect a State levee safety program under which a State levee safety agency, in accordance with State law, carries out the guidelines established under subsection (c)(1); and

children.label: B
children.text: allocate sufficient funds in the budget of that State to carry out that State levee safety program.

children.heading: Work plans
children.label: 3 Work plans
children.text: The Secretary shall enter into an agreement with each State receiving technical assistance under this subsection to develop a work plan necessary for the State levee safety program of that State to reach a level of program performance that meets the guidelines established under subsection (c)(1).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary, in coordination with the Administrator, shall carry out public education and awareness efforts relating to the levee safety initiative.

children.label: A
children.text: educate individuals living in leveed areas regarding the risks of living in those areas; and

children.label: B
children.text: promote consistency in the transmission of information regarding levees among Federal agencies and regarding risk communication at the State and local levels.

children.heading: State, regional, and tribal levee safety program
children.label: g State, regional, and tribal levee safety program

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , in consultation with the Administrator, the Secretary shall issue guidelines that establish the minimum components necessary for recognition of a State, regional, or tribal levee safety program as a participating program.December 16, 2016

children.label: i
children.text: has the authority to participate in the levee safety initiative;

children.label: ii
children.text: can receive funds under this chapter;

children.label: iii
children.text: has adopted any levee safety guidelines developed under this chapter;

children.label: iv
children.text: will carry out levee inspections;

children.label: v
children.text: will carry out, consistent with applicable requirements, flood risk management and any emergency action planning procedures the Secretary determines to be necessary relating to levees;

children.label: vi
children.text: will carry out public education and awareness activities consistent with the efforts carried out under subsection (f); and

children.label: vii
children.text: will collect and share information regarding the location and condition of levees, including for inclusion in the national levee database.

children.label: i
children.text: issue draft guidelines for public comment; and

children.label: ii
children.text: consider any comments received in the development of final guidelines.

children.heading: Assistance to States, regional districts, and Indian tribes
children.label: 2 Assistance to States, regional districts, and Indian tribes

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: A Establishment
children.text: The Administrator may provide assistance, subject to the availability of funding specified in appropriations Acts for Federal Emergency Management Agency activities pursuant to this chapter and subject to amounts available under subparagraph (E), to States, regional districts, and Indian tribes in establishing participating programs, conducting levee inventories, and improving levee safety programs in accordance with subparagraph (B).

children.label: i
children.text: meet the requirements of a participating program established by the guidelines issued under paragraph (1);

children.label: ii
children.text: use not less than 25 percent of any amounts received to identify and assess non-Federal levees within the State or regional district or on land of the Indian tribe;

children.label: iii
children.text: submit to the Secretary and Administrator any information collected by the State, regional district, or Indian tribe in carrying out this subsection for inclusion in the national levee safety database; and

children.label: iv
children.text: identify actions to address hazard mitigation activities associated with levees and leveed areas identified in the hazard mitigation plan of the State approved by the Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency under the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 5121

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Administrator shall implement quantifiable performance measures and metrics to assess the effectiveness of the assistance provided in accordance with subparagraph (A).June 10, 2014

children.label: I
children.text: ensures that human lives and property that are protected by new and existing levees are safe;

children.label: II
children.text: encourages the use of appropriate engineering policies, procedures, and technical practices for levee site investigation, design, construction, operation and maintenance, inspection, assessment, and emergency preparedness;

children.label: III
children.text: develops and supports public education and awareness projects to increase public acceptance and support of levee safety programs and provide information;

children.label: IV
children.text: builds public awareness of the residual risks associated with living in levee protected areas; and

children.label: V
children.text: develops technical assistance materials, seminars, and guidelines to improve the security of levees of the United States.

children.heading: Maintenance of effort
children.label: D Maintenance of effort
children.text: Technical assistance or grants may not be provided to a State under this subsection during a fiscal year unless the State enters into an agreement with the Administrator to ensure that the State will maintain during that fiscal year aggregate expenditures for programs to ensure levee safety that equal or exceed the average annual level of such expenditures for the State for the 2 fiscal years preceding that fiscal year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Administrator to carry out this subsection $25,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2019 through 2030.

children.label: I
children.text: ⅓ among States, regional districts, and Indian tribes that qualify for assistance under this subsection.

children.label: aa
children.text: the miles of levees in the State or regional district or on the land of the Indian tribe that are listed on the inventory of levees; bears to

children.label: bb
children.text: the miles of levees in all States and regional districts and on the land of all Indian tribes that are in the national levee database.

children.heading: Maximum amount of allocation
children.label: iii Maximum amount of allocation
children.text: The amounts allocated to a State, regional district, or Indian tribe under this subparagraph shall not exceed 50 percent of the reasonable cost of implementing the State, regional, or tribal levee safety program.

children.heading: Prohibition
children.label: F Prohibition
children.text: No amounts made available to the Administrator under this chapter shall be used for levee construction, rehabilitation, repair, operations, or maintenance.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall provide assistance to States, regional districts, Indian tribes, and local governments relating to addressing flood mitigation and levee rehabilitation activities that result in an overall reduction in flood risk.

children.label: A
children.text: participate in, and comply with, all applicable Federal floodplain management and flood insurance programs;

children.label: i
children.text: includes all levee risks; and

children.label: ii
children.text: complies with the Disaster Mitigation Act of 2000 (; );Public Law 106–390114 Stat. 1552

children.label: C
children.text: submit to the Secretary an application at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary may require;

children.label: D
children.text: commit to provide normal operation and maintenance of the project for the 50 year-period following completion of rehabilitation; and

children.label: i
children.text: acts in accordance with the guidelines developed under subsection (c); and

children.label: ii
children.text: carries out activities relating to the public in the leveed area in accordance with the hazard mitigation plan described in subparagraph (B).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 1 year after the date of execution of a project agreement for assistance under this subsection, a State, regional district, Indian tribe, or local government shall prepare a floodplain management plan in accordance with the guidelines under subparagraph (D) to reduce the impacts of future flood events in each applicable leveed area.

children.label: i
children.text: potential measures, practices, and policies to reduce loss of life, injuries, damage to property and facilities, public expenditures, and other adverse impacts of flooding in each applicable leveed area;

children.label: ii
children.text: plans for flood fighting and evacuation; and

children.label: iii
children.text: public education and awareness of flood risks.

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: C Implementation
children.text: Not later than 1 year after the date of completion of construction of the applicable project, a floodplain management plan prepared under subparagraph (A) shall be implemented.

children.heading: Guidelines
children.label: D Guidelines
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary, in consultation with the Administrator, shall develop such guidelines for the preparation of floodplain management plans prepared under this paragraph as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.December 16, 2016

children.heading: Technical support
children.label: E Technical support
children.text: The Secretary may provide technical support for the development and implementation of floodplain management plans prepared under this paragraph.

children.label: i
children.text: for any rehabilitation activity to maximize overall risk reduction associated with a levee under a participating State, regional, or tribal levee safety program; and

children.label: ii
children.text: only for a levee that is not federally operated and maintained.

children.label: i
children.text: to perform routine operation or maintenance for a levee; or

children.label: ii
children.text: to make any modification to a levee that does not result in an improvement to public safety.

children.heading: No proprietary interest
children.label: 5 No proprietary interest
children.text: A contract for assistance provided under this subsection shall not be considered to confer any proprietary interest on the United States.

children.heading: Cost share
children.label: 6 Cost share
children.text: The maximum Federal share of the cost of any assistance provided under this subsection shall be 65 percent.

children.heading: Project limit
children.label: 7 Project limit
children.text: The maximum amount of Federal assistance for a project under this subsection shall be $25,000,000.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 8 Limitation
children.text: A project shall not receive Federal assistance under this subsection more than 1 time.

children.heading: Federal interest
children.label: 9 Federal interest
children.text: For a project that is not a project eligible for rehabilitation assistance under , the Secretary shall determine that the proposed rehabilitation is in the Federal interest prior to providing assistance for such rehabilitation.section 701n of this title

children.heading: Other laws
children.label: 10 Other laws
children.text: Assistance provided under this subsection shall be subject to all applicable laws (including regulations) that apply to the construction of a civil works project of the Corps of Engineers.

children.heading: Prioritization
children.label: 11 Prioritization
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, the Secretary shall prioritize the provision of assistance under this subsection to economically disadvantaged communities (as defined by the Secretary under section 160 of the Water Resources Development Act of 2020 ( note)), including economically disadvantaged communities located in urban and rural areas.33 U.S.C. 2201
affects the requirement under ( note; ); orsection 100226(b)(2) of Public Law 112–14142 U.S.C. 4101126 Stat. 942

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary; or

children.label: B
children.text: the Administrator, including for the purpose of setting premium rates under the national flood insurance program established under chapter 1 of the National Flood Insurance Act of 1968 ( et seq.).142 U.S.C. 4011So in original. Probably should be “chapter I”.1
